title: Climate-resilient airport infrastructure planning
summary: Climate projections help airports anticipate heat, storm and flood impacts on capacity and infrastructure.
problem: Aircraft take-off performance falls as temperatures rise, and airport drainage and pavements face increasing climate stress. Infrastructure planning based on historical climate statistics underestimates future heat-restriction days and extreme-weather frequency.
solution: C3S climate projections and ERA5 reanalysis provide downscaled temperature, precipitation and extreme-event statistics to mid-century. These feed aircraft-performance modelling, drainage design and master-plan capacity assumptions, allowing airports to plan adaptation proactively.

copernicusRole.detail: C3S projections and ERA5 are downscaled and fed into aircraft-performance and drainage models. Copernicus is the climate input to an engineering modelling chain, not a ready-made answer.
